title: Lenzing Group Young Scientist Award 2026: €5,000 Prize for Innovative Fiber Research
summary: Applications are now open for the Lenzing Group Young Scientist Award 2026 , an international award recognizing outstanding academic research in the field of fiber innovation. The award is organized by Lenzing Group , a global leader in sustainable fiber production, and aims to support emerging researchers whose work contributes to the future of advanced materials and sustainable textile technologies. This year’s theme focuses on “High-Performance Cellulosics: Premium Fibers for Next-Generation Applications.” The competition highlights innovations that advance performance, sustainability, and circularity within the textile and nonwoven industries. About the Young Scientist Award The Young Scientist Award celebrates groundbreaking research that pushes the boundaries of fiber technology and sustainable materials. The program seeks academic work that demonstrates innovative approaches to developing high-performance cellulosic fibers. These materials play an increasingly important role in creating more sustainable and high-performing products across industries such as textiles, hygiene products, and technical applications. Through this award, the Lenzing Group aims to encourage young scientists to contribute research that supports sustainable manufacturing and the advancement of next-generation fiber technologies. Focus Area for 2026 The 2026 award focuses on research related to high-performance cellulosic fibers , which are increasingly being used to develop premium materials for advanced applications. Research topics may include innovations that enhance fiber performance, improve sustainability and recyclability, or enable new applications within the textile and nonwovens industries. Projects that demonstrate strong scientific methodology, innovation, and practical industry relevance will be particularly competitive. Award Benefits Selected researchers will receive recognition and opportunities to present their work to leading experts in the fiber industry. Benefits include: Prize funding of up to €5,000 Industry exposure through engagement with global fiber experts Opportunity to present research to a jury of industry and academic leaders Recognition for innovative work in sustainable fiber development The award provides an excellent platform for young researchers to showcase their work and build connections within the global textile and materials innovation community. Why This Award Matters The demand for sustainable and high-performance materials continues to grow across industries. Research in advanced fiber technologies plays a key role in developing more environmentally responsible solutions. The Young Scientist Award supports emerging researchers who are contributing to these advancements and encourages collaboration between academia and industry. Submission Deadline Researchers interested in participating must submit their applications by July 12, 2026 . Early submission is encouraged to ensure all application materials are successfully received and reviewed. How to Apply Applicants can submit their research through the official application portal. For more Award opportunities, visit here. The post Lenzing Group Young Scientist Award 2026: €5,000 Prize for Innovative Fiber Research appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: AstraZeneca Young Health Programme Impact Fellowship 2026: Global Opportunity for Young Leaders Advancing Health Equity
summary: Applications are now open for the AstraZeneca Young Health Programme Impact Fellowship 2026 , a prestigious global initiative designed to support young leaders working to improve health outcomes in underserved communities. The fellowship is supported by AstraZeneca in partnership with One Young World , bringing together passionate youth advocates who are tackling health challenges and strengthening healthcare systems worldwide. This fellowship provides selected leaders with funding, global exposure, leadership development opportunities, and the chance to attend one of the world’s largest gatherings of young changemakers. About the Young Health Programme Impact Fellowship The Young Health Programme Impact Fellowship focuses on empowering young leaders who are actively working to improve youth health and well-being. The initiative supports projects that address pressing health challenges, including strengthening health system resilience, advancing health equity, and tackling health issues linked to climate change. By providing resources and international visibility, the fellowship aims to accelerate impactful solutions that improve health outcomes in underserved communities. Fellowship Benefits Selected fellows will receive significant support to advance their initiatives and strengthen their leadership capabilities. Key benefits include: A fully funded scholarship to attend the One Young World Summit 2026 Participation in the global summit held in Cape Town , South Africa Access to global leadership training and mentorship opportunities Membership in the international One Young World network of changemakers Grant funding of $10,000 or $50,000 to implement or scale health-focused projects The fellowship aims to help young leaders expand the reach and effectiveness of their initiatives while connecting them with a powerful global community. Who Should Apply The program is designed for young leaders who are already making an impact in the health sector, particularly those focused on improving health outcomes for young people in underserved communities. Applicants should be founders or leaders of registered nonprofit organizations and demonstrate a strong commitment to advancing health equity and community well-being. Impact Areas Projects supported by the fellowship may focus on areas such as: Strengthening health systems resilience Advancing equitable access to healthcare Addressing climate-related health challenges Improving youth health outcomes in underserved communities The fellowship prioritizes initiatives that have the potential to create measurable and sustainable impact. Application Deadline The deadline to apply for the AstraZeneca Young Health Programme Impact Fellowship is April 12, 2026 . Interested candidates are encouraged to submit their applications before the deadline to be considered. How to Apply Applicants can learn more and submit their application through the official link . For more Fellowship Opportunities, Visit OFY Here. title: GirlTREK Joy and Justice Academy Development Partner RFP 2026: Funding Opportunity for Advocacy and Training Organizations
summary: GirlTREK has announced a Request for Proposals (RFP) for organizations interested in partnering to develop the Joy and Justice Academy , a new advocacy and organizing training initiative designed to empower Black women to address community challenges and influence policy change. The initiative is led by GirlTREK , a national health movement focused on improving life expectancy and quality of life for Black women through community engagement, walking campaigns, and civic action. Organizations with experience in advocacy, organizing, curriculum design, and training implementation are invited to submit proposals to help design and develop this important program. About the Joy and Justice Academy The Joy and Justice Academy is part of a broader strategic initiative launched by GirlTREK in 2025 to strengthen policy advocacy, civic engagement, and place-based organizing. The program aims to equip members of the GirlTREK community with practical skills and knowledge that enable them to tackle local challenges and advocate for healthier and more equitable communities. Through structured training and organizing support, the academy will help participants engage in policy discussions, community organizing, and grassroots advocacy. The program aligns with GirlTREK’s broader mission and its 10-Point Plan for Joy and Justice , which focuses on improving the health, safety, and well-being of Black women. Purpose of the RFP GirlTREK is seeking a qualified development partner that can collaborate with the organization to design and implement the training program. The selected partner will help create a curriculum and training framework that supports GirlTREK members in developing the skills necessary for advocacy and civic engagement. This partnership will contribute to building stronger community leadership and advancing initiatives that improve quality of life at the local level. Scope of Work The selected partner organization will play a key role in shaping and implementing the Joy and Justice Academy. Responsibilities may include: Designing the curriculum and training modules for the academy Supporting advocacy and community organizing training programs Developing capacity-building strategies for participants Implementing training and facilitating workshops Providing guidance on effective advocacy and policy engagement The program is expected to combine education, organizing strategies, and leadership development. Who Should Apply GirlTREK is looking for organizations with strong expertise in: Advocacy and policy engagement Community organizing Curriculum design and development Capacity-building and leadership training Training implementation and facilitation Organizations that have experience working with grassroots movements, civic engagement initiatives, or social justice programs may be particularly well suited for this opportunity. Why This Opportunity Matters The Joy and Justice Academy represents a significant effort to empower communities with the knowledge and tools needed to influence policy and improve public health outcomes. By equipping participants with advocacy and organizing skills, the program seeks to create a network of leaders who can address issues affecting Black women and their communities. The initiative reflects GirlTREK’s commitment to building sustainable pathways for community leadership, civic participation, and social change. Application Deadline Organizations interested in partnering with GirlTREK must submit their proposals by April 17, 2026 . Applicants are encouraged to review the complete RFP details in the official documentation before submitting their proposals. Applicants should carefully review the full RFP document for detailed application guidelines and submission requirements How to Apply Interested organizations should submit their proposal according to the instructions outlined in the RFP document. For questions or additional information, applicants can contact : advocacy@girltrek.org For More Policy Opportunities , Visit OFY here . title: WTCI Albrecht Fellowship 2026: Global Business Fellowship for Students and Recent Graduates in the United States
summary: Applications are now open for the WTCI Albrecht Fellowship 2026 , an immersive global business fellowship designed for college students and recent graduates interested in building careers in international trade and global markets. The program is organized by World Trade Center Institute (WTCI) and provides participants with exclusive access to global companies, leadership workshops, and a strong professional network within the Mid-Atlantic business community. Over the course of 10 days, selected fellows will gain firsthand exposure to how global companies operate and how international trade impacts industries and economies. About the Albrecht Fellowship The Albrecht Fellowship is a highly competitive program designed to help young professionals move beyond classroom learning and gain real-world insights into global business and international trade. Participants will meet executives, industry leaders, and policymakers while participating in workshops and networking sessions that focus on leadership, diplomacy, and cross-cultural collaboration. The fellowship creates opportunities for students and graduates to understand the practical dynamics of global business while building relationships with professionals who are shaping the future of international trade. Program Experience The fellowship offers an intensive and interactive learning experience that combines site visits, leadership workshops, and networking opportunities. Participants will go behind the scenes at influential global organizations in the Mid-Atlantic region, learning directly from executives and business leaders. Some companies fellows may interact with include Under Armour , McCormick Company , and Ciena . This exposure allows participants to explore how global companies manage international operations, innovation, and cross-border collaboration. Program Benefits Participants in the Albrecht Fellowship gain valuable professional experience and access to industry leaders. Benefits of the program include: Direct access to executives and decision-makers in global companies Leadership workshops focused on diplomacy, personal branding, and negotiation Networking opportunities with peers and mentors interested in international trade Exposure to global organizations and the international business ecosystem The fellowship is designed to help participants develop the skills, confidence, and networks needed to succeed in global careers. Program Dates and Location Program dates: August 11 – August 20, 2026 Location: Baltimore and the Mid-Atlantic Region, United States The program is conducted in person, allowing fellows to interact directly with companies, mentors, and fellow participants. Priority Application Deadline Applicants who submit their applications early can receive priority consideration. Priority deadline: April 25, 2026 Candidates are encouraged to apply early due to the competitive nature of the program. How to Apply Interested applicants can learn more and submit their applications through the official link For More Fellowship Opportunities, Visit OFY Here. title: UNCCD COP17 Media Reporting Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for Journalists to Report from Mongolia
summary: Journalists around the world now have an exciting opportunity to attend a major global environmental event through the UNCCD COP17 Media Reporting Fellowship . Organized by the United Nations Convention to Combat Desertification (UNCCD) , the fellowship supports journalists who want to cover international discussions on land restoration, climate change, and sustainable development. Selected fellows will receive funding to attend COP17 , which will take place in Ulaanbaatar , the capital city of Mongolia . This fellowship is designed to strengthen global media coverage of desertification, land degradation, and drought—issues that affect millions of people worldwide. About the UNCCD COP17 Media Fellowship The Media Reporting Fellowship provides journalists with the opportunity to attend the UNCCD Conference of the Parties (COP17), one of the world’s most important global gatherings focused on land sustainability and environmental resilience. Through the fellowship, journalists gain access to the conference, expert discussions, and global leaders working to combat desertification and promote sustainable land management. The program aims to amplify accurate and impactful reporting on environmental challenges and solutions, particularly those affecting vulnerable communities. What the Fellowship Offers Selected journalists will benefit from several forms of support that make it easier to report directly from the conference. Benefits may include: Travel support to attend COP17 in Mongolia Accommodation during the conference Access to conference sessions and media briefings Opportunities to interview experts, policymakers, and global leaders International media exposure and professional networking By attending COP17, fellows will gain firsthand insights into global environmental policy discussions and emerging solutions to land degradation and climate challenges. Who Should Apply The fellowship is designed for professional journalists who are passionate about environmental reporting and global development issues. Ideal candidates include reporters, editors, multimedia journalists, and correspondents interested in covering topics such as climate change, land restoration, drought, and sustainable development. Applicants should demonstrate a track record of journalism work and an interest in producing stories that raise awareness about environmental challenges and solutions. Why This Fellowship Matters Environmental reporting plays a critical role in informing the public and influencing global action. Programs like the UNCCD Media Fellowship help ensure that key international environmental discussions receive strong and informed media coverage. By supporting journalists to attend COP17, the fellowship helps strengthen global storytelling on land sustainability and climate resilience while empowering reporters to bring important environmental stories to audiences around the world. How to Apply Interested journalists must submit their application via email. Application instructions: Send your application and required documents to: press@unccd.int Subject line: COP17 Media Fellowship Application Applicants are encouraged to carefully review the full application requirements before submitting their materials. Learn more and read the full call for applications here: For more Fellowship opportunities, Visit OFY here. title: AI Sandbox Seed Grant 2026: $3,000 Funding for African Human Rights Organizations Building Ethical AI Solutions
summary: Civil society organizations and human rights defenders across Africa now have a powerful opportunity to integrate artificial intelligence into their advocacy work through the AI Sandbox Seed Grant 2026 . Launched by Code for Africa (CfA) as part of the Digitalise Youth consortium , this initiative offers seed funding and technical support to help organizations develop AI-driven solutions addressing some of the most pressing challenges facing human rights defenders in the digital age. The program aims to empower organizations that are working to defend civic freedoms, combat disinformation, and protect vulnerable communities from digital threats. Through the AI Sandbox Seed Grant, selected organizations will receive funding, mentorship, and technical assistance to prototype or scale innovative AI solutions that strengthen digital rights and civic engagement . Applications for this opportunity are open until March 30, 2026 , and successful organizations will join a collaborative cohort focused on ethical AI innovation across Africa. Empowering Human Rights Defenders Through AI Across Africa, human rights defenders increasingly operate in complex digital environments where misinformation campaigns, online harassment, surveillance, and censorship can undermine civic engagement and democratic participation. The AI Sandbox Seed Grant was created to help organizations address these challenges by leveraging artificial intelligence tools and techniques that strengthen their ability to monitor digital threats, verify information, and mobilize communities safely. Through this program, participating organizations will build minimum viable products (MVPs) or working prototypes designed to tackle digital challenges affecting civic space and human rights advocacy. These AI-powered tools will enable organizations to work more efficiently while protecting activists, journalists, and communities from digital harm. Grant Amount and Duration The AI Sandbox Seed Grant provides targeted funding designed to help organizations build and test practical AI solutions. Each selected organization will receive $3,000 in seed funding to support the development of their project. This funding may be used to support internal technology teams or to hire external AI expertise necessary to build the proposed solution. The grant duration is six months , allowing organizations to design, prototype, and test their AI-driven tools. Projects demonstrating strong progress, measurable impact, and commitment to long-term sustainability may also be considered for extensions beyond the initial six-month period . Who the Program Targets The program specifically targets Human Rights Defender (HRD) organizations and civil society organizations operating in the Sahel region and neighboring countries in West and East Africa . Particular priority will be given to organizations working in restrictive or hostile digital environments , where activists face heightened risks such as digital surveillance, censorship, or coordinated misinformation campaigns. The program seeks organizations with a demonstrated commitment to human-centered innovation , ensuring that the technologies developed respond directly to community needs and protect fundamental rights. Focus Areas for AI Innovation The AI Sandbox Seed Grant encourages applicants to develop solutions across several strategic areas related to digital rights and civic protection. Analysis and Monitoring Tools Organizations may develop AI-powered systems capable of analyzing digital discourse and tracking patterns of harmful content online. These tools can help monitor civic conversations, detect hate speech, identify coordinated misinformation campaigns, and analyze threats to freedom of expression across social media platforms. Such technologies can be particularly useful for monitoring elections, civic movements, and public debates . Applicants may also propose lightweight sentiment analysis tools capable of detecting trends in online discussions without requiring extensive computing infrastructure. Verification and Fact-Checking Solutions Another important focus area is the development of AI tools that help organizations verify information and detect manipulated content. These solutions may include tools designed to: Identify manipulated images or videos Detect misleading narratives online Support grassroots fact-checking initiatives Enable community-based verification efforts Such tools are especially valuable in contexts where disinformation campaigns are used to target human rights defenders, journalists, or civic organizations. Collaboration and Civic Engagement Tools The program also encourages the development of secure digital tools that allow human rights defenders and youth activists to collaborate safely. These tools may facilitate information sharing, coordination of advocacy campaigns, or community mobilization while protecting users from digital surveillance or harassment. Inclusive Access Solutions Projects that expand access to digital tools for women, marginalized communities, and underserved groups are also strongly encouraged. Such solutions might focus on bridging digital divides by making AI tools more accessible and inclusive. Technical Support and Mentorship One of the most valuable aspects of the AI Sandbox Seed Grant is the extensive technical support available to selected organizations. Grantees will receive mentorship and guidance from Code for Africa’s internal technical teams , including: TechLab DataLab AI Sandbox These teams will provide hands-on support in areas such as: AI development Cybersecurity best practices Data privacy protection Scalable system design In addition to technical support, participating organizations will benefit from strategic guidance from partners within the Digitalise Youth consortium , including policy experts, governance specialists, and research institutions. Networking and Global Visibility Organizations selected for the program will also gain access to a wide network of technology experts, policymakers, and human rights advocates. Through the Digitalise Youth project, grantees will have opportunities to collaborate with other organizations and participate in knowledge-sharing initiatives. Successful projects may also be showcased at major global forums such as: RightsCon The Internet Governance Forum The Summit for Democracy These platforms provide valuable opportunities for organizations to highlight their innovations, build partnerships, and influence international discussions on digital rights. Eligibility Requirements Organizations interested in applying for the AI Sandbox Seed Grant must meet several eligibility criteria. Applicants must: Be a registered non-profit organization or civil society organization Have a clear mandate to defend human rights Be based in the Sahel region or neighboring countries in West or East Africa Have a dedicated technology team or a clear plan to hire AI expertise Demonstrate a clear need for AI-based tools to address challenges such as disinformation, hate speech, or digital surveillance Commit to ethical AI practices , including transparency, fairness, accountability, and protection of user data Demonstrate community engagement and participatory design in the development of the proposed solution The Digitalise Youth Project The AI Sandbox Seed Grant is part of the Digitalise Youth Project , an initiative within the broader Digital Democracy Initiative . The project seeks to address shrinking civic space and the spread of disinformation across the Sahel and surrounding regions by empowering youth activists and civil society organizations with digital skills and civic technology tools. By strengthening the relationship between human rights defenders and the technology community, the project helps organizations navigate the digital ecosystem, protect themselves from surveillance, and promote responsible online engagement. Key Partners Supporting the Initiative The Digitalise Youth project is supported by a coalition of organizations working to advance digital rights, civic engagement, and democratic governance. Key partners include: Code for Africa (CfA) AfricTivistes European Partnership for Democracy (EPD) Canal France International (CFI) World Scout Bureau Africa (WOSM) Kofi Annan Foundation (KAF) Each partner contributes expertise ranging from digital activism and media integrity to governance reform and youth capacity building. Final Thoughts The AI Sandbox Seed Grant offers a unique opportunity for African civil society organizations to integrate cutting-edge technology into their human rights work. By combining seed funding, technical mentorship, and international visibility, the program empowers organizations to build tools that strengthen digital rights and protect civic space. Human rights organizations operating in challenging digital environments are strongly encouraged to apply before the March 30, 2026 deadline . Find more information here Discover more opportunities here The post AI Sandbox Seed Grant 2026: $3,000 Funding for African Human Rights Organizations Building Ethical AI Solutions appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Disability Rights Fund 2026 Grant Round for Sub-Saharan Africa: Funding Opportunities for Organizations of Persons with Disabilities
summary: Organizations of Persons with Disabilities (OPDs) across Sub-Saharan Africa often face significant challenges in securing sustainable funding for their advocacy, policy work, and community-driven initiatives. Many OPDs rely heavily on volunteer commitment and grassroots support to advance disability rights, making access to dedicated funding both essential and transformative. One of the most significant opportunities for disability-led organizations is the upcoming 2026 grant round from the Disability Rights Fund (DRF) . This grant opportunity provides critical financial support to organizations led by persons with disabilities, enabling them to advocate for rights, influence policies, and strengthen disability inclusion efforts across the continent. As the 2026 grant cycle approaches, eligible organizations should begin preparing early to maximize their chances of securing funding. What Is the Disability Rights Fund? Founded in 2008 , the Disability Rights Fund is a pooled grantmaking mechanism dedicated exclusively to supporting organizations led by persons with disabilities . The fund works alongside its sister organization, the Disability Rights Advocacy Fund (DRAF) , which specifically supports advocacy initiatives related to legislative change and policy reform. Together, DRF and DRAF have provided funding to hundreds of OPDs across several regions, including: Sub-Saharan Africa South Asia Southeast Asia Haiti Pacific Island Countries A defining feature of the Disability Rights Fund is its governance structure. Persons with disabilities are not merely beneficiaries of the grants—they are actively involved in decision-making. Members of the disability community participate in the Grantmaking Committee , ensuring that funding decisions reflect the priorities and lived experiences of people with disabilities. This governance model reflects the principle widely embraced within the disability rights movement: “Nothing about us without us.” Transparency is another key pillar of the DRF’s operations. Since its creation, the organization has maintained a public directory tracking every grant awarded from 2008 through 2024 , reinforcing accountability to the disability rights movement and its partners. Overview of the 2026 Grant Round The Disability Rights Fund continues to expand its support for disability-led organizations. A major development in recent grant cycles has been the expansion of eligibility to all countries across Sub-Saharan Africa , moving beyond earlier geographic limitations. The 2026 grant round is expected to follow a similar structure to previous cycles and will likely include three main funding streams designed to support different types of organizations and coalitions. Small Grants Small grants are designed to support local or sub-national organizations of persons with disabilities that are working directly within communities. These grants can provide up to USD 60,000 over two years , helping smaller OPDs strengthen advocacy campaigns, implement programs, and expand their organizational capacity. Mid-Level Coalition Grants Mid-level coalition grants support collaborative initiatives involving three or more organizations . These grants recognize that collective action can amplify the impact of disability advocacy efforts. Coalitions can receive between USD 30,000 and USD 50,000 per year to support coordinated advocacy campaigns, policy engagement, and strategic initiatives. National Coalition Grants National coalition grants are designed for larger national-level partnerships advocating for systemic change. These grants provide USD 40,000 to USD 60,000 per year , with a total potential funding amount of up to USD 120,000 over two years . By supporting coalitions, DRF encourages collaborative efforts that strengthen the disability rights movement at national and regional levels. Application Process: Letter of Interest (LOI) Applications for the Disability Rights Fund begin with a Letter of Interest (LOI) submission. This initial stage allows organizations to present their proposed project and organizational profile before being invited to submit a full proposal. LOI forms are made accessible in multiple languages , including: English French Portuguese Indonesian The availability of forms in French is particularly important for Francophone African organizations , ensuring that language barriers do not prevent participation in the grant process. Eligibility Requirements The Disability Rights Fund has clear eligibility criteria to ensure that funding directly benefits disability-led organizations. To qualify, an applicant organization must: Be headquartered and primarily operating in a Sub-Saharan African country targeted by DRF Be a registered organization of persons with disabilities (OPD) Alternatively operate under a fiscal sponsor that is a registered OPD Propose a project aligned with the United Nations Convention on the Rights of Persons with Disabilities (CRPD) DRF emphasizes projects that promote implementation and monitoring of the CRPD at local, national, or regional levels. Activities That Are Not Funded It is important for applicants to understand what types of activities are not eligible for DRF funding . The fund does not support : Individual applicants Construction projects or building accessibility upgrades Direct service provision programs Income generation initiatives Legislative lobbying activities (these fall under DRAF funding streams) Organizations applying for coalition grants must also ensure that the lead organization is an OPD , although coalition partners may include other civil society organizations working on human rights or social justice. Timeline for the 2026 Grant Cycle Organizations planning to apply should carefully note the key dates for the 2026 grant round. Portal opens for LOI submissions: May 30, 2026 Deadline for Letters of Interest: June 21, 2026 at 23:59 ET Invitations for successful LOIs: July 28, 2026 Deadline for full applications (invited organizations only): August 23, 2026 Final grant decisions announced: December 2026 Successful projects begin: January 1, 2027 Preparing well in advance is strongly recommended, as previous grant rounds have received extremely high numbers of applications. Multi-Year Funding: A Major Advantage for OPDs One of the most impactful changes introduced by the Disability Rights Fund in recent years is the option for multi-year funding . Beginning with the 2023 grant cycle , eligible returning grantees can apply for two years of funding through a single proposal process . This change addresses a major challenge faced by many disability organizations: the administrative burden of annual grant cycles. By providing multi-year funding, DRF allows organizations to focus more time and energy on advocacy and program implementation rather than constant fundraising. Multi-year funding also enables organizations to: Plan longer-term advocacy campaigns Hire staff with greater job security Strengthen internal governance and operational capacity Preparing a Strong Letter of Interest Given the competitive nature of the DRF grants, organizations should begin preparing their applications early. Strong LOIs typically include the following elements: Clear alignment with the CRPD Applicants should identify the specific articles of the Convention on the Rights of Persons with Disabilities that their project supports. Demonstration of disability-led leadership DRF prioritizes organizations where persons with disabilities occupy leadership positions and decision-making roles . Strategic advocacy focus Projects linked to global disability advocacy initiatives—such as commitments made through the Global Disability Summit —can strengthen an application. Organizational documentation Applicants should prepare essential documents in advance, including: Organizational background information Financial statements Governance documentation Organizations that are not formally registered should also identify a fiscal sponsor that is a registered OPD . Additional Funding Opportunities for Disability Organizations While preparing for the DRF grant round, organizations may also explore other funding opportunities that support disability inclusion. Several international foundations and development institutions provide funding for disability-related initiatives, including: Open Society Foundations Ford Foundation CBM Global African Development Bank Platforms such as ReliefWeb and Devex Opportunities also provide listings of disability-related grants and development funding opportunities. Final Thoughts The Disability Rights Fund represents one of the most important funding mechanisms for organizations led by persons with disabilities in Sub-Saharan Africa. By supporting advocacy aligned with the Convention on the Rights of Persons with Disabilities, the fund strengthens the global disability rights movement and empowers local organizations to influence policies and advance inclusion. As the 2026 grant round approaches , organizations should begin preparing their documentation, strengthening partnerships with other OPDs, and aligning their advocacy strategies with international disability rights frameworks. The opportunity is designed specifically for disability-led organizations working to advance equality, inclusion, and human rights across Africa. For more information click here Discover more opportunities here The post Disability Rights Fund 2026 Grant Round for Sub-Saharan Africa: Funding Opportunities for Organizations of Persons with Disabilities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Alan W. Heston Fellowship 2026: Up to $10,000 to Support Research in Global Economic Measurement and Purchasing Power Parity
summary: Emerging scholars and practitioners working in the field of economic measurement now have an exceptional opportunity to advance their research through the Alan W. Heston Fellowship 2026 . This prestigious fellowship program is designed to support innovative research related to international comparisons of prices, real incomes, purchasing power parity (PPP) methods, and the statistical data used to compare economies across countries . The fellowship honors the legacy of Professor Alan W. Heston , a leading figure in the field of international economic statistics whose work helped shape global approaches to measuring economic performance and living standards. Through this fellowship, a new generation of researchers is encouraged to build upon this legacy by contributing innovative insights and advancing the tools used to measure economic realities around the world. Applications for the fellowship are open from March 1 to March 29, 2026 , and selected fellows can receive up to USD 10,000 to support their research over a period of six to twelve months . The Vision Behind the Alan W. Heston Fellowship The Alan W. Heston Fellowship aims to empower scholars and professionals who are committed to improving how economic data is collected, analyzed, and compared globally. Economic measurement plays a crucial role in shaping public policy, international development strategies, and global economic research. By supporting projects that strengthen economic measurement methodologies, the fellowship contributes to improving the reliability and transparency of international economic statistics. Accurate data on price levels, income comparisons, and purchasing power parity are essential for understanding economic disparities, evaluating living standards, and informing international cooperation. The fellowship therefore encourages research that contributes to better measurement tools and deeper analytical frameworks for cross-country economic comparisons. Research Focus Areas Applicants are encouraged to propose research projects that align with the fellowship’s core themes. These themes are rooted in the fields of international economic statistics and comparative economic analysis. Key areas of focus include: International Comparisons of Prices Projects may explore methodologies for comparing price levels across countries, helping economists better understand differences in the cost of living and economic purchasing power between nations. Real Income Comparisons Research in this area examines how real income levels differ globally when adjusted for price differences. Understanding real income variations is essential for evaluating global inequality and development progress. Purchasing Power Parity (PPP) Methods Purchasing power parity is a fundamental concept in international economics. The fellowship supports research that refines PPP estimation methods or improves the reliability of PPP data used in global economic comparisons. Data for Cross-Country Comparisons Accurate international economic comparisons depend on robust and reliable data systems. Research may focus on improving data quality, expanding datasets, or developing new approaches to collecting and interpreting international economic statistics. Many research projects supported by the fellowship may also relate to the work of the International Comparison Program (ICP) , a global statistical initiative that produces internationally comparable price and economic data. Fellowship Duration and Structure The Alan W. Heston Fellowship offers a flexible structure designed to accommodate a variety of research timelines and professional commitments. The fellowship term ranges from six to twelve months , allowing recipients sufficient time to conduct in-depth research, develop methodologies, analyze data, and produce meaningful academic or policy-oriented outputs. During this period, fellows are expected to work on research projects that contribute to the advancement of economic measurement and international statistical methodologies. Fellowship Funding Selected fellows may receive up to USD 10,000 in funding to support their research activities. The fellowship funding can be used to support various research-related expenses, such as: Data collection and acquisition Research assistance Statistical analysis tools Travel related to research collaboration or data access Time allocated to research and writing By providing financial support, the fellowship helps scholars dedicate time and resources to advancing critical research in international economic statistics. Honoring the Legacy of Professor Alan W. Heston The fellowship is named in honor of Professor Alan W. Heston , whose pioneering contributions significantly shaped modern international economic measurement. Professor Heston played a critical role in the development of methods used to compare economies across countries. His work contributed to improving the accuracy of purchasing power parity calculations and global economic datasets, which are now widely used by economists, policymakers, and international organizations. Through the fellowship program, his legacy continues to inspire new research that strengthens global economic understanding. Why Economic Measurement Research Matters Economic measurement may seem highly technical, but it has profound implications for global development and policy-making. Reliable economic statistics help governments and international organizations: Compare living standards across countries Understand global inequality Design effective development policies Measure economic progress accurately Evaluate international economic performance Improving the tools used for international economic comparisons ensures that policymakers and researchers can rely on accurate information when making critical decisions. By supporting scholars working in this field, the Alan W. Heston Fellowship contributes to strengthening the global knowledge base that underpins economic policy and international cooperation. Application Timeline Applicants should carefully note the fellowship application window. Applications open on March 1, 2026 , and must be submitted by March 29, 2026 . Prospective fellows are encouraged to prepare their research proposals early to ensure they present a clear research objective, methodology, and expected contribution to the field of economic measurement. Final Thoughts The Alan W. Heston Fellowship represents a unique opportunity for emerging scholars and practitioners to contribute to one of the most important areas of international economic research. By supporting innovative research on purchasing power parity, price comparisons, and global economic statistics, the fellowship strengthens the tools used to understand the world’s economies. Researchers who are passionate about improving economic measurement and advancing international statistical methods are strongly encouraged to apply before the March 29, 2026 deadline . For more information click here APPLY HERE Discover more opportunities here The post Alan W. Heston Fellowship 2026: Up to $10,000 to Support Research in Global Economic Measurement and Purchasing Power Parity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: YouthMADE Festival Amplify Grant 2026: $500 Funding Opportunity for Student-Led Projects to Expand Innovation and Impact
summary: Students with innovative ideas and impactful projects now have the opportunity to receive financial support through the YouthMADE Festival Amplify Grant 2026 . Sponsored by Ciena , this grant program provides $500 grants to students participating in the YouthMADE Festival , enabling them to amplify, expand, and showcase their projects in meaningful ways. The Amplify Grant is designed to empower student creators, innovators, and changemakers who are developing projects that demonstrate creativity, leadership, and social impact. By offering financial support, the program ensures that students can access the resources they need to grow their ideas, share their work with broader audiences, and deepen the impact of their initiatives. Applications for the 2026 YouthMADE Festival Amplify Grant are open and will remain available until April 12, 2026 . What Is the YouthMADE Festival Amplify Grant? The YouthMADE Festival Amplify Grant is a financial award created to support student-led projects that demonstrate creativity, originality, and positive impact. The program encourages students to develop projects that address real-world issues, highlight underrepresented voices, and showcase innovative approaches to learning and problem-solving. By providing funding, the Amplify Grant allows students to take their projects to the next level. Whether the goal is to complete a project, expand its reach, or present it to new audiences, the grant supports students in turning their ideas into tangible outcomes. This initiative reflects a broader commitment to empowering youth as leaders and innovators. Students are encouraged not only to design projects but also to actively lead them, taking responsibility for planning, implementation, and communication. How the Grant Funding Can Be Used Recipients of the YouthMADE Festival Amplify Grant can use the $500 funding in various ways to strengthen and share their projects. The grant is flexible and can support several types of project-related expenses. Eligible uses of the grant include: Purchasing supplies and materials Students may use the grant to buy materials needed to complete or expand their projects. This may include craft supplies, technology components, research materials, or other resources required to bring the project to completion. Acquiring tools, licenses, or equipment Funding may also be used to obtain digital tools, software licenses, or equipment that helps students document and share their work. This could include video production tools, design software, recording equipment, or other resources that allow students to present their projects effectively. Hosting events to showcase student work Students may use grant funds to organize events that highlight their projects as part of the YouthMADE Festival. These events might include exhibitions, presentations, demonstrations, or community showcases where students share their work with peers, educators, and community members. Presenting projects at conferences Another eligible use of the grant is covering costs associated with presenting student projects at conferences or similar events. This could include registration fees, presentation materials, or other expenses necessary for sharing the project with broader audiences. Participating in workshops or training opportunities Students can also use the funding to attend workshops, training programs, or educational sessions that help them develop skills related to their projects. This could include technical training, storytelling workshops, leadership development sessions, or other learning opportunities. The grant program is designed to encourage both creativity and professional development, enabling students to strengthen their skills while expanding their projects. Important Funding Distribution Details One important aspect of the Amplify Grant program is how the funds are distributed. Rather than being paid directly to individual students, the grant funds are disbursed to an educational organization connected to the student. This organization could include: A public school A private school A registered homeschool program A legally recognized educational organization such as a museum An after-school program A youth agency or similar institution The receiving organization agrees to manage the funds and ensure that they are used in accordance with the purposes outlined in the grant application. This approach ensures transparency, accountability, and proper management of the grant funding. Evaluation Criteria for Applications Applications for the YouthMADE Festival Amplify Grant are carefully reviewed and evaluated by a panel that includes students and members of the Digital Promise team . The review process focuses on several key criteria to identify projects that demonstrate strong leadership, innovation, and impact. Student Leadership One of the most important evaluation criteria is that the project must be student-led . Students should take the primary role in designing and implementing the project. Educators may act as mentors, advisors, or facilitators, but they should not be the individuals directing or creating the project itself. This criterion ensures that the initiative remains a platform for youth leadership and creativity. Clarity and Value of the Project Applicants must clearly explain their project. The application should describe: What the project is about How the project works or functions Why the project was created Additionally, students must explain the impact of the project , either current or potential. This includes describing how the project benefits others, addresses an issue, or contributes to learning or community engagement. Clear and Feasible Use of Grant Funds The application must include a detailed plan describing how the $500 grant will be used. Reviewers assess whether the proposed use of funds is clear, relevant to the project, and feasible within the scope of the grant. A strong proposal demonstrates how the funding will directly help expand or share the project. Authenticity and Uniqueness The selection panel also looks for projects that demonstrate originality and authenticity. This may include projects that: Amplify underrepresented voices Address issues from non-dominant perspectives Offer new approaches to familiar challenges Showcase creativity and innovation For example, a project might explore disaster preparedness through the lens of disability inclusion or examine environmental issues from the perspective of marginalized communities. The program also strongly encourages applications from students who have not previously received awards or recognition for their work. Commitment to Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion Projects that incorporate principles of diversity, equity, and inclusion are particularly encouraged. This may involve promoting inclusive storytelling, addressing social challenges, or creating spaces where diverse voices can be heard. The program recognizes that innovative ideas often come from perspectives that are not traditionally represented. How to Apply Students interested in receiving the YouthMADE Festival Amplify Grant must submit their application through the official application process. Applicants are encouraged to review the Amplify Grant overview and terms , which provide additional details about eligibility requirements, expectations, and guidelines for the use of funds. Students can also attend the YouthMADE Festival information session , where they can learn more about the program, ask questions, and receive guidance on preparing a strong application. For further assistance, applicants may also consult the program’s Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) or contact the organizers via email. Learn From Previous Recipients Prospective applicants can gain inspiration by exploring projects developed by previous YouthMADE Festival Amplify Grant recipients . These examples demonstrate the wide range of creative, impactful, and innovative ideas that the grant program supports. From community initiatives to technological prototypes and creative storytelling projects, the Amplify Grant has supported students in transforming their ideas into meaningful outcomes. Final Thoughts The YouthMADE Festival Amplify Grant represents an important opportunity for students to take ownership of their ideas and transform them into impactful projects. By providing financial support, mentorship opportunities, and a platform for sharing student work, the program encourages young innovators to lead change in their communities. Students who are passionate about their projects and eager to expand their impact are strongly encouraged to apply before the April 12, 2026 deadline . Find more information here APPLY HERE Discover more opportunities here The post YouthMADE Festival Amplify Grant 2026: $500 Funding Opportunity for Student-Led Projects to Expand Innovation and Impact appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: ScottishPower Master Scholarship 2026–2027
summary: The ScottishPower Master Scholarship 2026–2027 is one of the most prestigious fully funded postgraduate opportunities in the United Kingdom, designed to develop the next generation of innovators in engineering, technology, and energy-related fields. Offered by ScottishPower , this scholarship provides not only full financial support but also direct industry exposure, mentorship, and career pathways in the rapidly evolving global energy sector. About the ScottishPower Master Scholarship The ScottishPower Master Scholarship is aimed at nurturing highly motivated individuals who want to contribute to transforming the global energy landscape. The program focuses on key future-oriented sectors such as renewable energy, engineering innovation, artificial intelligence, and digital infrastructure. Scholars are supported throughout their academic journey and are also integrated into a professional ecosystem that prepares them for long-term careers in the energy industry. Fields of Study Covered The scholarship supports postgraduate programs in high-demand and future-focused disciplines, including: Engineering and Energy-Related Fields Electrical Engineering Mechanical Engineering Civil Engineering Electronic Engineering Technology and Digital Fields Cybersecurity Data Science and Business Analytics Artificial Intelligence (AI) Cloud Computing Telecommunications These fields are specifically chosen to align with global energy transformation and digital innovation trends. Participating Universities in the UK ScottishPower collaborates with top universities in the UK, including: University of Edinburgh University of Cambridge Imperial College London University of Glasgow University of Strathclyde Heriot-Watt University Applicants may also apply to any UK university, provided the chosen course aligns with the scholarship’s eligible fields. Scholarship Benefits (What You Will Receive) The ScottishPower Master Scholarship is a fully funded opportunity, covering all major academic and living expenses: Full payment of university tuition fees Monthly stipend of £1,200 (approximately £14,400 annually) Dedicated industry mentor Career development and employment opportunities Exposure to real-world industry projects This comprehensive funding ensures that students can focus entirely on their studies and professional growth without financial burden. Who Can Apply? (Eligible Candidates) Eligible Category of Applicants Graduates and final-year undergraduate students Individuals passionate about energy, sustainability, and technology Candidates seeking postgraduate (Master’s) studies Eligible Countries Open to all nationalities (global applicants), including: Africa Asia Europe North America South America Oceania International students are fully eligible to apply. Academic and General Requirements To qualify for the scholarship, applicants must: Hold or expect to obtain a Bachelor’s degree with at least a 2:1 (or equivalent) Apply and secure admission to a relevant UK master’s program Demonstrate strong academic performance and motivation Show interest in innovation and the energy sector Additionally: Applicants must be able to attend an in-person assessment in the UK if shortlisted Previous recipients of ScottishPower or related group scholarships are not eligible Responsibilities of Selected Scholars Successful candidates are expected to: Act as ambassadors for STEM and the energy industry Promote ScottishPower career opportunities Participate in recruitment and outreach activities This ensures that scholars actively contribute to the broader academic and professional community. Application Process (Step-by-Step) The selection process is highly competitive and includes multiple stages: 1. Online Application Submit your application via the official ScottishPower scholarship portal. 2. Online Assessment Video interview Game-based assessments 3. Assessment Centre In-person evaluation in the UK (Glasgow or Hoylake) 4. Final Selection Successful candidates are notified in June 2026. Apply here Duration of the Scholarship Typically covers one-year full-time master’s programs in the UK Why You Should Apply This scholarship is more than financial aid—it is a career-launching platform. Key advantages include: Direct connection to a leading energy company Access to mentorship and real-world projects Strong employability prospects after graduation Opportunity to study at globally ranked UK universities Deadline 29th March 2026 (2 PM UK time) The ScottishPower Master Scholarship 2026–2027 stands out as a highly competitive and rewarding opportunity for students who aspire to build impactful careers in engineering, technology, and sustainable energy. With full funding, industry mentorship, and access to top-tier UK institutions, it is an ideal pathway for ambitious students worldwide.
